name: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago
mission: Teaching and reading of the holy Quran, daily and Friday congregations, Islamic Education for Children and perform marriages and funeral services
theoryOfChange: By providing a combination of daily worship, full-time and part-time Islamic education, and social services (Zakat/Clinic), the organization aims to develop moral and responsible leaders and maintain economic balance in the Muslim community.
scoreSummary: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago shows good impact and moderate alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs (ISNS) explicitly accepts zakat donations, stating, "Donate your ZAKAT to ISNS" and providing instructions to "Mail your zakat donation check to the following address: 3950 Industrial Avenue Rolling Meadows, IL 60008, USA." They also mention a "Zakah drop box in the Masjid" and list "Zakat Services" on their website. Additionally, "Zakath" is a selectable category for online donations. (Source: https://isns.org/services/community-services/zakat/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 117 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

amalEvaluation.charity_name: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 27/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Zakat-collecting organization holds 117 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

risks.description: Zakat-collecting organization holds 117 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago shows good impact and moderate alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs (ISNS) explicitly accepts zakat donations, stating, "Donate your ZAKAT to ISNS" and providing instructions to "Mail your zakat donation check to the following address: 3950 Industrial Avenue Rolling Meadows, IL 60008, USA." They also mention a "Zakah drop box in the Masjid" and list "Zakat Services" on their website. Additionally, "Zakath" is a selectable category for online donations. (Source: https://isns.org/services/community-services/zakat/)

all_citations.claim: The organization reports a program expense ratio of 92.5%, fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised, and 117.3 months of working capital.
The charity is zakat-eligible and maintains a specific policy for zakat collection and distribution.
Total revenue for the period was $3,697,122, supporting programs such as daily prayers, Sunday school, and funeral services.
The organization provides multiple giving methods for donors to support its religious and community programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates excellent financial health and efficiency, though the overall score is moderated by medium data confidence and the need for more detailed impact reporting.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance public disclosure of specific impact metrics and beneficiary outcomes
Increase transparency regarding third-party program evaluations

dimension_explanations.alignment: ISNS offers a high fit for Muslim donors as a zakat-eligible institution that adheres to traditional asnaf categories for distribution [2][4]. The organization addresses urgent religious and cultural needs through its Sunday school and daily prayer programs [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The society shows strong capacity-building potential with a total revenue of $3,697,122 and a high program expense ratio of 92.5% [1][3]. While financial health is excellent with 117.3 months of working capital, more specific data on beneficiary outcomes would further validate its direct impact [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago serves as a vital community hub providing comprehensive religious services, education, and social support.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 92.5% ensures the vast majority of funds directly support community services
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.01 per $1 raised
Robust financial stability with 117.3 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago (ISNS) is a religious non-profit dedicated to the teaching of the Holy Quran, daily congregational prayers, and Islamic education for children [1][3]. The organization facilitates community welfare through zakat distribution and provides essential services including funeral arrangements and marriage ceremonies [2][3].
